Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a POWL model with anomalies and asked to 1 Identify the anomalies in the given POWL model 2 Generate hypotheses on why these anomalies might exist 3 Propose database queries to verify these hypotheses using the provided tables

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: However note the instruction The query must visibly and directly demonstrate the required Anomaly Detection in Process Models using Event Logs and Process Mining

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Since the user asks for a response that includes the three parts but the final output should be SQL queries The instruction says Your task is to 1 Identify 2 Generate 3 Propose what could be done to verify using the underlying database Suggest how one might write database queries

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But the overall system prompt says Write PostgreSQL SQL for the instruction using only the provided database context

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This is conflicting Lets read carefully Your task is to 1 Identify 2 Generate 3 Propose

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Perhaps the intended output is the SQL queries that would be used to verify the hypotheses part 3

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So we are to write SQL queries that detect the anomalies in the event log

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Given the anomalies Loop between E and P multiple evaluations and approvals for same claim XOR that may skip N claims closed without notification Partial ordering allowing C before loop completes claims closed without evaluation or approval

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Well write SQL queries for 1 Claims closed without evaluation or approval 2 Claims with multiple approval events indicating loop 3 Claims closed without notification 4 Claims where close event occurs before evaluation or approval

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Note The event log has activity labels R A E P N C as per the model We assume these are the values in claim_events activity

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Well use CTEs to get event sequences per claim

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Since the instruction says Return only the final SQL no explanations well output a single SQL block with multiple queries maybe separated by semicolons or a single query that returns multiple results Usually we can provide multiple statements

Name: Validation - IND
Text: However the system says Prefer a single statement unless multiple are essential We can provide multiple statements if needed

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets create a set of queries that can be run independently
